name: Gao Changgong

skills.name: Secret Skill - Rush
skills.description: When an enemy is within a certain range from Prince of Lanling, moving toward them will grant him increased Movement Speed.

skills.name: Secret Skill - Split
skills.description: Deals damage to enemies within a certain range, and deals a 2nd strike of damage to a random enemy in range.

skills.name: Secret Skill - Sling
skills.description: Throws a dagger that damages, slows, and marks the first enemy in its path. When attacking marked targets, stuns them and recovers Health. Marks deal grievous damage when disappearing.

skills.name: Secret Skill - Strike
skills.description: Exits stealth mode and dashes in the target direction, dealing damage to enemies in his path.

tips: Gao Changgong is a Medium Assassin (Jungling) on Honor of Kings Global. Camp HOK ranks them Tier A with 49.2% win rate and 0.5% pick rate as of 2026-06-05.
Best build path: Relentless Blade → Boots of Resistance → Meteor → Axe of Torment → Rapacious Bite → Master Sword.
Run Penetration for stats that match Gao Changgong's Assassin kit. Take Flash & Execute — swap to Purify against heavy CC or Smite when jungling.
Standard trade: Secret Skill - Split → Secret Skill - Sling → Secret Skill - Strike → reposition. Weave basic attacks between skill casts for maximum damage and passive value.
Laning: Clear to level four, then gank the lane with the least escape. Secure Tyrant when your bot or mid has crash wave.
Teamfight: Wait for CC from your team, then dive carry with full combo. Exit with reset or flash if focus fired.
Gao Changgong excels into Angela, Daji, Cai Yan. Avoid blind picking into Athena, Hou Yi, Daji without team peel.

Gao Changgong is a viable Tier A pick in high rank with 49.2% win rate — strong in one-trick hands or as a counter-pick. Ban rate 0.7% — expect Gao Changgong to be removed in tryhard lobbies; have a backup in the same role.

faqs.question: Is Gao Changgong good in the current meta?
faqs.answer: Gao Changgong is Tier A Assassin with 49.2% win rate, 0.5% pick rate, and 0.7% ban rate on Honor of Kings Global (Camp HOK, 2026-06-05). Solid situational pick.

faqs.question: What is the best Gao Changgong build?
faqs.answer: Core item path: Relentless Blade, Boots of Resistance, Meteor, Axe of Torment, Rapacious Bite, Master Sword. Run Penetration arcana with Flash & Execute. Switch to alternate build presets on this page for jungle, clash, or late-game setups.

faqs.question: How to counter Gao Changgong?
faqs.answer: Pick Athena, Hou Yi, Daji into Gao Changgong. Deny Jungling resources early and force fights before Gao Changgong completes core items. Gao Changgong struggles when the enemy can peel and group them.

faqs.question: Who does Gao Changgong counter?
faqs.answer: Gao Changgong is strong into Angela, Daji, Cai Yan according to HoKStats matchup data and role matchups on the global server. Draft Gao Changgong when the enemy relies on these heroes in the Jungling lane.

faqs.question: Is Gao Changgong good in high rank / Diamond+?
faqs.answer: Gao Changgong is a viable Tier A pick in high rank with 49.2% win rate — strong in one-trick hands or as a counter-pick. Ban rate 0.7% — expect Gao Changgong to be removed in tryhard lobbies; have a backup in the same role.

faqs.question: What lane should Gao Changgong play?
faqs.answer: Gao Changgong is tagged Jungling in Camp export (Assassin). Clear to level four, then gank the lane with the least escape. Secure Tyrant when your bot or mid has crash wave.

faqs.question: Best arcana and spells for Gao Changgong?
faqs.answer: Recommended: Penetration arcana. Battle spell: Flash & Execute. Adjust Purify/Execute based on enemy dive and jungle need.

faqs.question: Gao Changgong or Wukong — which is better?
faqs.answer: Gao Changgong vs Wukong (Assassin, Tier S): Wukong is 1.3% pick / 49.7% WR. Wukong wins 0.5% more often globally. Choose Gao Changgong when you need Jungling identity; choose Wukong for Jungling comfort.

faqs.question: Should I ban Gao Changgong?
faqs.answer: Yes in tryhard queues — 0.7% ban rate. Deny if enemy first-picks Assassin.
